##NAMES=Chalcophanite
##RRUFFID=R050451
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=ZnMn^4+^_3_O_7_·3H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Morenci mine, Morenci, Greenlee County, Arizona, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=University of Arizona Mineral Museum
##DESCRIPTION=A mass of small black hexagonal plates, not homogeneous
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R050451
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Zn_0.60_Fe^2+^_0.20_Cu_0.12_Fe^3+^_0.08_)(Mn^4+^_2.92_Fe^3+^_0.08_)O_7_·3H_2_O ; = bright phase. The gray phase is jacobsite (Mn_0.65_Zn_0.25_Cu_0.09_)(Fe^3+^_1.58_Mn^3+^_0.42_)O_4_  dark phase: K-feldspar
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder, cell parameters are from single crystal
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 7.55 b: 7.55 c: 20.86 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 1029 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
